title: Jimp Numbers
description:
A positive integer k is called a jimp number if there is exactly one triple of positive integers (a, b, c) such that a, b, and c are consecutive terms of an arithmetic progression (exactly in that order) and a2 + b2 + k = c2.

Count the jimp numbers which are not greater than the given number n.
input: The only line contains a single integer n (1 ≤ n ≤ 1011).
output: Print a single integer: the answer to the problem.
source: Camp, Petrozavodsk Programming Camp, Winter 2019, Day 6: Belarusian SU Contest, Contest, Open Cup, 2018/2019 Season, Stage 12: Grand Prix of Belarus
